w:r.w:t: Antigravity-Ready SOP / Manual for Chrome Extension Automation
Professional SOP + AI Coding-Agent Execution Contract
F2V / Frames-to-Video using Start/End frame UI
PARTIALLY VERIFIED from uploaded screenshot/chart only; exact DOM selectors are NOT VERIFIED

Purpose: convert the rough visual SOP into a deterministic implementation manual that Antigravity can use to build, test, and repair the Google Flow Chrome extension without guessing UI order, user intent, or pass/fail criteria.
Create a new Flow project session.
Screenshot 1 verifies New Project button exists. Exact selector NOT VERIFIED.
Open creation-type selector and select Video.
Screenshot 2 verifies Image/Video toggle. Exact active state NOT VERIFIED.
Screenshot 2 verifies Frames tab. Ingredients/I2V not in this SOP.
Screenshot 2 verifies visible Veo 3.1 - Lite label.
Screenshot 2 shows Start and End upload slots. End-frame behavior NOT VERIFIED by source step.
Insert prompt into field: “What do you want to create?”
Open project menu after generation/project state.
Screenshot 3 verifies menu opened from 3-dot icon.
Screenshot 3 verifies Download Project menu item.
All required chips show selected state
Upload start frame, then inject prompt
Image slot populated and prompt field contains exact text
Job enters generating/submitted state; no validation error
Browser download starts or project export confirmation appears

Build or repair the Chrome Extension automation flow for Google Flow F2V/Frames mode so it can configure the UI, upload image input, inject prompt text, submit the job, and download the finished project using deterministic state checks.
F2V / Frames-to-Video only: New Project, Video, Frames, 9:16, 1x, Veo 3.1 - Lite, start-frame upload, prompt injection, submit, project download.
Ingredients mode, Image mode, I2V subject/scene/style multi-image workflow, bulk queueing, account/pro credit purchasing, and undocumented DOM manipulation.

Do not hardcode brittle pixel coordinates as the primary locator method. Use DOM-accessible selectors, visible text, aria labels, roles, and stable attributes first. Pixel or image matching may be used only as a fallback with explicit logging.

Exact Google Flow DOM selectors, file input accept pattern, async generation lifecycle, and download completion events are NOT VERIFIED by the provided workbook. Antigravity must inspect live DOM and record proof before finalizing selectors.
Visible text match with normalized casing
Sibling of Image toggle in settings popover
File input linked to Start slot
Click Start slot then attach file to active input
Enabled submit button near prompt box
Button with arrow icon in composer footer
Button aria-label/menu trigger near project top bar
First menu item with download icon
Flow page loaded and extension active
Frames UI with Start/End slots visible
Set prompt value and dispatch input/change events
Project generated or project menu available
Load unpacked extension and open Google Flow
Extension service worker active; content script injected
Run F2V automation with one local image + one prompt
Refresh already-open Flow tab after extension reload
No stale content script crash; reinjection succeeds
FAIL if automation needs manual page reopen
At least text/aria locator plus fallback per target
FAIL if only pixel coordinates used
Inserted prompt equals source string byte-for-byte
FAIL if prompt truncates or mutates
Start slot visibly filled or DOM preview detected
FAIL if file selection not reflected
Generation state or queue state detected
FAIL if click fires with no UI transition
Chrome downloads API or browser event confirms start/completion
FAIL if only menu click is logged
Every step must log: state ID, locator used, action attempted, before/after screenshot hash or DOM proof, and timestamp.
If a locator fails, attempt exactly one fallback locator family before aborting. Do not loop blindly.
If the Google Flow UI changes, abort with ERR_SELECTOR_DRIFT and attach current DOM snapshot plus screenshot.
If upload fails, do not submit. Abort with ERR_IMAGE_UPLOAD_FAILED and include file path, file size, and slot state proof.
If prompt injection fails, do not submit. Abort with ERR_PROMPT_INJECTION_FAILED and include actual field value.
If generation is slow, wait by explicit UI state or network/download event, not by fixed sleep only.

Build or repair the Chrome Extension automation for Google Flow Frames-to-Video mode based on the attached SOP. Implement deterministic UI automation for: New Project → Video → Frames → 9:16 → 1x → Veo 3.1 - Lite → upload Start frame → inject prompt → submit → 3-dot menu → Download Project.
Target: Chrome Extension Manifest V3, local unpacked extension, Google Flow UI.
Do not implement Image, Ingredients, bulk queueing, or unrelated modes unless existing code already supports them and no regression is introduced.

Inspect manifest.json, background/service worker, content scripts, injected/page scripts, popup/options UI, automation runner, messaging bridge, download handler, and tests. Do not assume file names. Find actual entry points from manifest.json.

Use DOM-accessible selectors first: role, aria-label, visible text, placeholder, stable attributes. Do not use brittle pixel coordinates as primary selectors. For every UI step, implement an assertion that proves the expected state changed before moving to the next step. Add bounded fallback locators and explicit abort codes.
S0_READY, S1_PROJECT_OPEN, S2_MODE_SET, S3_CONFIG_SET, S4_IMAGE_READY, S5_PROMPT_READY, S6_SUBMITTED, S7_DOWNLOADABLE, S8_EXPORTED.

Create or update deterministic tests covering selector resolution, state transitions, prompt exact-match injection, upload slot proof, submit proof, menu/download proof, and already-open tab reinjection after extension reload.

Return: exact files changed, git diff summary, test command, test output, screenshots/log snippets proving each state, commit SHA, and pass/fail status. If not committed, state NOT COMMITTED. If selectors are not verified against live Google Flow DOM, state NOT VERIFIED.
Automation completes all 11 SOP actions in order
Every selector has primary + fallback + assertion
FAIL if upload cannot be proven
Prompt field exact-match verified after injection
Download event confirmed, not merely clicked
Existing extension functions still pass tests
FAIL if unrelated working mode breaks
Document generated from uploaded workbook. Exact live Google Flow DOM remains NOT VERIFIED until Antigravity inspects the target page.
